contentList.content: Precision is the first step toward ruin. A single flaw, properly exploited, will unravel even the strongest form.
A fine haze clings to the air around the wearer, unseen but ever-present. Those who linger within it find their footing uncertain, their defenses quietly undone.
At its heart lies a reaction that cannot be halted—only delayed. Once begun, all things are reduced to their simplest, most obedient state.
Every touch leaves a trace, every trace a reaction. What begins as a stain soon becomes a spreading, choking bloom.
Where the wearer walks, the ground remembers. Each step feeds the current, and the current does not forget its path.
It does not beat—it circulates. A steady flow of perfected decay, sustaining its bearer long after lesser forms would fail.
Reactions fade when starved of energy. This one does not fade. It lingers, clings, and endures beyond its natural end.
To understand dissolution is to embrace it. The boundary between flesh and solvent grows thin… and then irrelevant.
A perfect medium for transmission. Through it, the reaction spreads—not wildly, but with deliberate, unerring purpose.
When the wearer deals[1], they also create a [2] pool of acid under the target(s).
Dealing[1] with your Spells or Cantrips has a chance to cause a gnarly <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_ACID_TRIP">Acid Trip</LSTag> for the target.
The wearer of these robes becomes the engine of dissolution, causing their foes to be afflicted with worsening phases of various acidic afflictions over time.
The effects of acid exposure are taking a toll. Attacks against the entity deal an additional [1].

With their <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_CORROSION">Corrosion</LSTag> left untreated, the entity hase entered the final stage of dissolution. They suffer an additional [1] from attacks and have their <LSTag Tooltip="MovementSpeed">movement speed</LSTag> halved. Futhermore, the severe degradation of their body's physical makeup leaves them <LSTag Tooltip="Vulnerable">Vulnerable</LSTag> to all damage.

Entity was unable to rid themselves of <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_EXPOSURE">Exposure</LSTag> and has begun to corrode. They suffer an additional [1] from attacks and have their <LSTag Tooltip="MovementSpeed">movement speed</LSTag> halved.
Give everyone a ton of temp hp for testing purposes

• If entity is not standing in acid at the start of their
turn, the exposure is cleansed.

• If entity cannot escape the acid before the exposure
expires, it must make a successful Constitution
saving throw or become afflicted with Corrosion.

• If entity receives any healing before the exposure
expires, the corrosion is cleansed.

• If entity is not healed before the corrosion expires, it
it must make a successful Constitution saving
throw or become afflicted with Dissolution.
The wearer and their allies are unaffected by <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="ACID">Acidic surfaces</LSTag> and their weapons are automatically dipped while standing in them.
Affected entity is standing in <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="ACID">Acid</LSTag>, and its weapon attacks deal an additional [1].
Affected entity's weapons are coated in acid and deal an additional [1].
This aura grants the entity and its allies immunity to <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="ACID">Acidic surfaces</LSTag> and their weapons are automatically dipped while standing in them.
Entity is unaffected by <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="ACID">Acidic surfaces</LSTag> and its weapons are automatically dipped while standing in them.

Pick a creature afflicted with <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_DISSOLUTION">Dissolution</LSTag> and cause them to begin <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_RUPTURING">Rupturing</LSTag>, dealing massive[1] to them and potentially starting a chain reaction of acidic death.

The dissolution of the entity's flesh has been catalyzed, causing them to take massive[1]. If they die in this state, they will become <LSTag Type="Spell" Tooltip="Projectile_CC_Ruptured>Ruptured</LSTag>, exploding and potentially cause a chain reaction.

All creatures within [1] of the rupturing creature take [2]. If any of those creatures are suffering from <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_DISSOLUTION">Dissolution</LSTag>, they begin <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_RUPTURING">Rupturing</LSTag>.
On a successful save, the creatures still take half damage.
Give a target dissolution to test.
Entity has been exposed to acid and begin to burn for [1] each turn.
Give a target corrosion to test.
Give a target exposure to test.

When the wearer deals[1] to any target under the influence of <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="CC_SurfaceExposure_Passive">Dissolution Protocol</LSTag>, they gain 1 <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_CHARGED_ACID">Caustic Charge</LSTag>.
Shoot [2] magical acid darts, possibly dealing [1] with each dart.

Gain 5 Temporary Hit Points per charge. Additionally, when hit by a melee attack, deal [1] equal to the total number of temporary hit points granted by the current number of charges. (e.g. - 3 charges = [2])

When the maximum number of charges is reached, consume them and gain an additional Action.
When hit by an attack, deal[1] equal to the total number of temporary hit points granted by your current number of Caustic Charges. (e.g. - 3 charges = [2])

While the wearer has <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_CHARGED_ACID">Caustic Charges</LSTag>, enemies standing in acid are inflicted with <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_DEX_DISADVANTAGE_DEBUFF">Reflex Erosion</LSTag>.
Standing in acid is slowly breaking down the entity's body, imposing <LSTag Tooltip="Disadvantage">Disadvantage</LSTag> on Dexterity <LSTag Tooltip="SavingThrow">saving throws</LSTag>.
Standing in acid has begun to slowly break down the entity's muscles, imposing <LSTag Tooltip="Disadvantage">disadvantage</LSTag> on Dexterity <LSTag Tooltip="SavingThrow">saving throws</LSTag>.

Phase 1: Enemies are afflicted with Exposure when they begin their turn standing in Acid.

Phase 2: Enemies are afflicted with Corrosion if Exposure expires and they fail a Constitution saving throw.

Phase 3: Enemies are afflicted with Dissolution if Corrosion expires and they fail a Constitution saving throw.

Once per turn, when an <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_EXPOSURE">Exposed</LSTag> or <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_CORROSION">Corroding</LSTag> creature fails any <LSTag Tooltip="SavingThrow">Saving Throw</LSTag> associated with the wearer's spells, reduce the countdown of Exposure or Corrosion by 1.
Reducing the countdown will cause the creature to reach the next phase of <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="CC_SurfaceExposure_Passive">Dissolution Protocol</LSTag> faster.
A tiny chest, perfect for tiny hands.
A common chest of timber and iron.
The wearer can cast an acid spell once per turn without expending a spell slot.
You can cast an acid spell once per turn without expending a spell slot.

When the wearer has <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_CHARGED_ACID">Caustic Charges</LSTag> they will make a Constitution <LSTag Tooltip="SavingThrow">Saving Throw</LSTag> at the end of their turn in an attempt to prevent the reduction of charges caused by the progression of turns.

Entity has made a Constitution <LSTag Tooltip="SavingThrow">Saving Throw</LSTag> in an attempt to prevent the reduction of their <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_CHARGED_ACID">Caustic Charges</LSTag> caused by turn progression.
The wearer's blood has been transmuted into a caustic agent. When hit in a way that causes them to spill blood, a small pool of acid forms at the wearer's feet.
The wearer gains +[1] to <LSTag Tooltip="SavingThrow">Saving Throws</LSTag> and <LSTag Tooltip="SpellSaveDC">Spell Save DC</LSTag>.
When the wearer starts their turn standing in acid, they draw power from it to gain 2 <LSTag Type="Status" Tooltip="CC_CHARGED_ACID">Caustic Charges</LSTag>.
On a successful save, the creature(s) take no damage.
Extra damage based on the current phase of <LSTag Type="Passive" Tooltip="CC_SurfaceExposure_Passive">Dissolution Protocol</LSTag> the entity is afflicted with.
Standing in acid has begun to slowly break down the entity's muscles, imposing <LSTag Tooltip="Disadvantage">disadvantage</LSTag> on Dexterity <LSTag Tooltip="SavingThrow">saving throws</LSTag>.

When one of your <LSTag Tooltip="Cantrip">cantrips</LSTag> deals[1], add your <LSTag Tooltip="SpellcastingAbilityModifier">Spellcasting Modifier</LSTag> and <LSTag Tooltip="ProficiencyBonus">Proficiency Bonus</LSTag> to the damage dealt.
Affected entity like totally can't control its actions and wanders around without direction.
